title: Add flag to log to stdout
body:
This PR simply adds a flag that allows users to configure if logrus logs to `stderr` (the default) or `stdout`. 

Logrus currently [cannot log to different locations based on the log levels](https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/issues/403) and defaults to logging all levels to `stderr`. This causes issues when systems are collecting these logs and reporting INFO logs as errors because they are logged to `stderr`. This provides a simple mechanism for changing that behavior.

Signed-off-by: Noah Krause <krausenoah@gmail.com>

comments.body: Thanks. Why is this extra flag necessary instead of just using shell’s redirection (`2>&1`, `&>`, `|&`)?

Changes LGTM
But if we go forward with this, we'll also need a man page change, I think here:https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/docs/skopeo.1.md
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
